$comment: Triaged advisories that audit.yml will not fail the build on. Each entry MUST include a reassess date. A PR that adds an entry here is a security-policy change — review accordingly.

advisories.title: Prototype Pollution in sheetJS
advisories.reason: SheetJS has not published xlsx to npm since 2023; the fix is only on their self-hosted CDN. We carry xlsx as a fallback parser path. The prototype pollution requires attacker-controlled property names in a workbook we then walk untyped — our read path materializes plain values, not arbitrary objects, so the gadget surface is small. Tracked for replacement.

advisories.title: SheetJS Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS)
advisories.reason: Same package as above; same npm-availability constraint. ReDoS is a CLI-availability concern only — xlsx-for-ai is a developer tool, not a server. A malicious workbook can hang the user's own CLI invocation; nothing crosses a trust boundary. Tracked for replacement alongside GHSA-4r6h-8v6p-xvw6.

advisories.title: uuid: Missing buffer bounds check in v3/v5/v6 when buf is provided
advisories.reason: Reaches us as a transitive of exceljs. Fix requires an exceljs major bump (currently ^4.4.0; advisory's fixAvailable points at 3.4.0 which is a regression). The vulnerable code path requires the caller to pass a pre-allocated buffer to uuid.v4() etc.; exceljs does not. Severity moderate, not blocking today's high+ gate — tracked here so the next exceljs major review consciously closes it.
